channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Brazilian President Lula swipes at Trump as US imposes auto tariffs
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva swiped Thursday at U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to raise U.S. tariffs on a wide range of products, saying Trump should consider the potential harm to the American and global economies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea's Kim oversees test of AI-powered suicide drones
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has overseen tests of newly developed AI-powered suicide drones and called for their increased production, North Korean state media said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A 'coalition of the willing' mulls more aid for Ukraine and a possible European armed force
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Ukraine’s allies from across Europe gathered for talks in Paris on Thursday about how to strengthen Kyiv’s hand and its military as it pushes for a ceasefire with Russia, and to consider proposals to deploy European troops in the country in tandem with any peace deal.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and SAMUEL PETREQUIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NATO clarifies comments that four missing US soldiers had died during training in Lithuania
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — NATO on Wednesday clarified comments that Secretary-General Mark Rutte made earlier in the day, when he suggested that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania had died, even though the U.S. Army said their fate was not yet confirmed.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea sent 3,000 more troops to Russia, according to South's assessment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea sent around 3,000 additional troops to Russia in January and February in continued support for Russian President Vladimir Putin’s war on Ukraine, South Korea’s military said Thursday in its latest assessment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron says a proposed European force for Ukraine could 'respond' if attacked by Russia
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron said Wednesday that a proposed European armed force for possible deployment in Ukraine in tandem with an eventual peace deal could “respond” to a Russian attack if Moscow launched one.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US gives initial approval for Qatar to purchase MQ-9 armed drones for nearly $2 billion
item.description: The United States has given an initial approval for the energy-rich Mideast nation of Qatar to buy eight armed MQ-9B Predator drones for its military, an estimated purchase worth nearly $2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France's foreign minister is in China to discuss Ukraine and trade issues
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot began a two-day visit to China on Thursday and held talks with his Chinese counterpart on Ukraine and longstanding trade disputes between China and Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Sudan opposition says its leader Machar has been arrested as UN warns of a renewed civil war
item.description: JUBA, South Sudan (AP) — South Sudan’s main opposition party said its leader Riek Machar has been arrested as the U.N. called on all parties to uphold the 2018 agreement that ended the country's civil war. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: DENG MACHOL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Brazil ex-President Bolsonaro will stand trial over an alleged coup plan. Here’s what happens next
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices unanimously ruled that former President Jair Bolsonaro and seven of his associates will stand trial on five counts, including attempting to stage a coup after the far-right leader lost the 2022 election.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea struggles to contain wildfires that have killed 27
item.description: Multiple wildfires raging across South Korea’s southern regions for days have killed 27 people and destroyed more than 300 structures, as thousands of personnel and dozens of helicopters have mobilized to battle the the county’s worst-ever blazes.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration to end funding for vaccinating children in poor countries
item.description: The Trump administration plans to end U.S. funding for Gavi, a global program that purchases shots to help vaccinate children in developing countries against some of the world’s deadliest diseases.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, The Hill

item.title: Sudan’s army chief returns to Khartoum for first time in 2 years of war after airport recaptured
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan's army said Wednesday it had recaptured Khartoum's international airport, and the military chief flew back to the capital for the first time in nearly two years of war, bringing the military closer to regaining full control of the city from the rival Rapid Support Forces paramilit
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN to reduce staff in Gaza, blaming Israel for a strike that killed its employee
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it will “reduce its footprint” in the Gaza Strip after an Israeli tank strike hit one of its compounds last week, killing one staffer from Bulgaria and wounding five other employees.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, EDITH M. LEDERER and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows 'highly skilled' pilot's final moments before fatal air show crash
item.description: South African pilot James O’Connell killed over the weekend in crash during air show at the Saldanha Bay Airfield, in an incident captured on video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean PM Han Duck-soo reinstated as acting president after impeachment overturned
item.description: South Korea's Constitutional Court overturned acting President Han Duck-soo's impeachment, reinstating the nation's No. 2 official as acting leader on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US peace talks with Ukraine, Russia get underway in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Discussions between U.S. and Russian delegations aimed at ending the war in Ukraine are underway Monday in Saudi Arabia, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist arrested, attacked by locals after scaling world-famous sacred Mayan temple, video shows
item.description: Video shows a tourist scaling the steps of Mexico's Temple of Kukulcan as thousands of visitors flocked to the site to witness the spring equinox last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is back home after a 5-week hospital stay for life-threatening double pneumonia
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A frail Pope Francis returned to the Vatican on Sunday after a five-week hospitalization for life-threatening double pneumonia, and he made a surprise stop at his favorite basilica on the way home before beginning two months of prescribed rest and recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, PAOLO SANTALUCIA and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey jails Istanbul mayor before trial, protests rage throughout city
item.description: A Turkish court jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, pending a trial on corruption charges, in a move likely to cause the country's biggest protests in more than a decade.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Canadian prime minister calls snap election to respond to Trump
item.description: New Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney kicked off his election campaign on Sunday ahead of the country's 45th federal election scheduled on April 28.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope returns to Vatican after 5-week hospital stay over life-threatening bout of pneumonia
item.description: A weak and frail Pope Francis returned home to the Vatican from the hospital on Sunday after surviving a five-week, life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: America's European allies try to pry unspent money back from USAID
item.description: Three European allies provided millions of dollars that the United States was supposed to spend for low-income countries.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, JAMEY KEATEN, MIKE CORDER AND VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis to be released from hospital Sunday after 5 weeks fighting life-threatening pneumonia
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday after 38 days battling a severe case of pneumonia in both lungs that threatened his life on two occasions and raised the prospect — for now put to rest — of a papal resignation or funeral.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, TRISHA THOMAS and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2 convicted in US of Iran-backed plot to kill journalist critical of Tehran
item.description: Prosecutors said Iran’s elite Revolutionary Guard Corps paid Amirov and Omarov $500,000 for the botched hit.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: UK issues travel warning for US
item.description: The United Kingdom updated its advice, warning of harsh consequences for British passport holders who violate U.S. immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Russian drones strike Ukrainian city of Odesa, underlining challenges for even limited truce
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drones pummeled the Ukrainian Black Sea port city of Odesa, injuring three people and sparking massive fires, officials said Friday, an attack that underlined Moscow's intention to pursue aerial strikes even as it agreed to temporarily halt strikes on energy facilities.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Military leaders discuss Ukraine peacekeeping force as partial ceasefire plans are worked out
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Senior military officers from more than 30 countries across Europe and beyond met in England on Thursday to flesh out plans for an international peacekeeping force for Ukraine as details of a partial ceasefire are worked out. U.K.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taliban release an American man who was abducted while traveling in Afghanistan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American man who was abducted more than two years ago while traveling through Afghanistan as a tourist has been released by the Taliban in a deal with the Trump administration that Qatari negotiators helped broker, the State Department said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US offers $15M reward for team accused of smuggling drone tech to Iran
item.description: Liu Baoxia, Li Yongxin, Yung Yiu Wa and Zhong Yanlai are accused of facilitating the sale of U.S. goods to front companies based in China.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation
